Product

Treat the air path. Improve the burn. Reduce waste.

NanoEFX is a passive, nanostructured treatment applied to your engine's air filter — or any suitable intake-filter medium. It is not a fuel additive, oil additive, ECU remap, mechanical retrofit or powered device. It's a water-based spray that dries into a thin, breathable nano-coating. As the engine draws air through that treated surface, the coating conditions the intake air before combustion — supporting a cleaner, more complete and more efficient burn.

What it is

  • A passive nanostructured intake-air treatment
  • Applied to the air filter or intake medium
  • Water-based — dries to a thin, breathable coating
  • Works before combustion, at the intake-air stage
  • REACH compliant · water-based

What it isn't

  • A fuel additive
  • An oil additive
  • An ECU remap or tuning
  • A mechanical retrofit or powered device

How it's applied — a spray, not a retrofit

Applied by your own team in minutes — no workshop, no parts, no downtime. How often it’s reapplied depends on the vehicle or engine (see below).

1

Remove the air filter

Access the existing air filter — the same one you already service.

2

Apply NanoEFX spray

Spray NanoEFX evenly onto the filter. The water-based mist carries it on and evaporates.

3

Let it dry & reinstall

Leave it to dry for at least 20 minutes, then refit the filter. No new hardware, no calibration, no mechanic.

4

Run the engine

Cleaner combustion from the very next start — with no change to how you operate.

No engine modification20–45 min to apply — varies by vehicle & engineCars, trucks & buses: reapply every ~5,000–12,000 kmShips & generators: calculated by run-hours & sizeNo downtime
See how it works →
How it works

Cleaner combustion, from the intake air

NanoEFX conditions the intake-air pathway before combustion. The water-based spray dries into a thin, breathable nano-coating on the filter — a passive functional surface. As air passes through, it interacts with the coating's polar mineral particles and oxygen-facing surface chemistry, supporting a cleaner, more complete and more stable burn — so more of every litre turns into useful work.

A passive surface interaction — no wires, batteries, electrodes or powered fields.
More complete combustion → lower fuel use and lower emissions.
Works with existing engines — no fuel or oil additive, no ECU tuning, no mechanical changes.
REACH compliant · water-based.

The ingredient system

The effect doesn't come from one material — it comes from how the full formulation builds a stable, breathable, functional coating on the filter surface.

1
Tourmaline nanoparticles

A polar mineral with a large surface area — it turns the filter from a passive barrier into a functional air-contact surface. No wires or power; the material itself interacts with airflow and humidity.

2
Nanosilver

Very small, highly surface-active silver particles that support the active, oxygen-facing character of the coating — working before combustion, not as a fuel or exhaust additive.

3
Silver-oxygen surface

A surface that interacts with the oxygen already present in intake air, helping prepare the air side of combustion. It doesn't add oxygen to the engine.

4
Porous mineral carriers

Build a larger, more consistent functional surface and distribute the active materials — while keeping the filter fully breathable.

5
Alginic-acid binder

Anchors the formulation to the filter medium so the coating stays thin, stable and breathable — it never chokes the engine.

6
Fucoidan

A marine-derived polysaccharide forming a moisture-responsive matrix around the active ingredients — because real intake air always carries humidity.

7
Hinoki oil & carriers

Help the treatment wet the filter and spread evenly, forming one consistent layer across the whole filter area.

8
Water-based delivery

Water carries the formulation onto the filter, then evaporates. What remains — and does the work — is the dried nano-coating.

What it does — a cleaner, more complete burn

Less wasted fuel — more complete combustion means lower fuel consumption.
Lower CO — more carbon is fully oxidised instead of leaving as carbon monoxide.
Fewer unburned hydrocarbons — less fuel slips through unburned.
Less soot & smoke — fewer fuel-rich zones during the burn.
Lower CO₂ — burning less fuel means less total CO₂.
Less carbon buildup — a cleaner burn forms fewer new deposits over time.

NanoEFX doesn't add energy or oxygen, and doesn't modify your engine — it helps the engine waste less of the energy already in the fuel. NOx response is engine- and control-system dependent.

Teaser · 2:19

A quick introduction

A short intro to NanoEFX's air-first approach to cleaner, more efficient combustion.

Deep dive · 7:23

Go deeper

The full technical walk-through — conditioning the air path, the ingredients and the effects.

Read the full technical explainer

The challenge. Real combustion is never perfect. Under changing load, temperature, humidity, fuel quality and engine age, some fuel never releases its full energy — showing up as CO, unburned hydrocarbons, soot, smoke, higher fuel use and carbon buildup. NanoEFX addresses this before combustion, at the intake-air stage.

What it does. Applied to the air filter, NanoEFX dries into a thin, breathable nano-coating. The filter still captures dust, but its surface now also acts as a passive nano-interface between the atmosphere and the engine. It doesn't add oxygen, replace air, force electricity into the intake, or change hardware — it conditions the intake-air pathway.

In the intake pathway. As air passes the treated surface it interacts with polar mineral particles (tourmaline), nanosilver and silver-oxygen chemistry, porous carriers and moisture-responsive components — all held by a stable binder. A useful analogy: people perform better breathing cleaner, more consistent air. NanoEFX helps the engine "breathe" through a treated surface designed for cleaner, more stable combustion.

Inside the engine. Better-prepared intake air can support more stable ignition, more consistent flame development and more complete fuel oxidation — reducing incomplete-burn zones and fuel waste. It doesn't override injection timing, boost or engine management; it supports the air side.

Emissions & efficiency. More complete combustion means less wasted fuel, less CO and hydrocarbons, less soot and smoke, and lower total CO₂ (because less fuel is burned — it doesn't remove carbon from fuel). Cleaner combustion also forms less new carbon buildup, easing maintenance over time. NOx is different: it depends on combustion temperature and the engine's own emissions-control strategy, so its response is engine-dependent.

Where it applies. Any combustion engine that breathes through a filter or suitable intake medium — trucks, buses, vans, marine, generators, construction, agriculture, mining, rail and more.

Treat the air path. Improve the burn. Reduce waste.

Where it works

Wherever an engine breathes through a filter

NanoEFX works on internal combustion engines where intake air passes through a treatable filter or intake-filter media — across diesel, petrol/gasoline, LPG/CNG, biofuel and many hybrid ICE applications.

Road transport

Very high

Heavy trucks, buses & coaches, vans, taxi & ride-share fleets, pickups and passenger cars.

Marine

Very high

Cargo ships & tankers, ferries, fishing vessels, tugs, offshore & auxiliary engines.

Power generation

Very high

Diesel & gas gensets, prime & backup power, CHP, irrigation and pump engines.

Construction & heavy equipment

Very high

Excavators, wheel loaders, dozers, dump trucks, cranes, pavers & compressors.

Agriculture

Very high

Tractors, combine & forage harvesters, sprayers, irrigation pumps & forestry machines.

Mining & quarrying

Very high

Haul trucks, crushers & screeners, drilling rigs, industrial pumps & compressors.

Rail

High

Diesel locomotives, diesel multiple units and rail-maintenance machines.

Port, airport & logistics

High

Reach stackers, terminal tractors, forklifts and airport ground-support equipment.

Military, emergency & public sector

High

Military vehicles, emergency gensets, fire trucks and municipal fleets.

Small engines & specialty

Medium

ATVs / UTVs, portable gensets, pumps and small petrol engines.

Works across engine & fuel types

Wherever there is suitable intake filtration — paper, PET, felt, washable or acoustic media that can be treated.

DieselPetrol / gasolineLPGCNG / LNGBiogasBiodieselHybrid ICE2-stroke4-strokeTurbochargedNaturally aspirated

Note: large marine two-stroke engines may not use a paper air filter — NanoEFX can still apply where PET, felt, washable, acoustic or other treatable intake media is present. Category images are placeholders; real photography to be added.

Business & ESG value

One treatment, two balance sheets

The savings land where it matters — on operating cost and on your emissions reporting.

For the CFO — lower fuel cost

Fuel is the largest controllable line in most fleets. A few percent off consumption compounds across every vehicle, every year — with no capex and no downtime.

  • Direct reduction in fuel spend
  • Lower exposure to EU ETS carbon cost (ETS2 from 2027)
  • No capital outlay — an operating treatment, not a retrofit

For ESG — lower emissions, documented

Less fuel burned means proportionally less CO₂ — litre for litre. We make no separate emissions claim; the CO₂ reduction follows directly from the fuel you save, and drops straight into your reporting cycles.

  • Lower CO₂, in direct proportion to fuel saved
  • Measured CO₂ reduction for Scope 1 reporting
  • Supports SDG-aligned sustainability documentation

Recognised sustainability — and a badge for your vehicles

NanoEFX is recognised by UNIDO (United Nations Industrial Development Organization) and aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals. Vehicles treated with NanoEFX can carry an SDG badge sticker — visible proof of your commitment to cleaner operation.

Request SDG badges →

Company · About us

Our story

Reducing emissions — one engine at a time.

“We have the wisdom to protect the Earth by reducing emissions — one engine at a time.”

Our green promise

In a world grappling with global warming, air pollution, dwindling flora, ozone-layer depletion and deforestation, we inadvertently contribute to environmental crises through the emissions we release. Combustion engines — vital to our economy and daily life — play a decisive role.

We are committed to tackling these challenges with sustainable solutions. With our eco-friendly treatment we reduce emissions and contribute to a cleaner world, one engine at a time — and our commitment reaches far beyond Japan, across the globe.

Now is the moment to heed the Earth’s subtle calls and respond with impactful action.

Milestones

2009Company established in Japan.
2010–11Prototype developed and first test sales.
2014Featured in the Emirates in-flight magazine.
2016Listed on the UNIDO Environmental Technology Database.
2017Adopted by the Audi Japan Racing Team.
2018Patent registered; international distribution begins.
2022–25EU roll-out and exclusive distribution across Scandinavia, India and the Philippines.

The leadership behind NanoEFX

Introducing the minds steering our sustainable revolution.

Chie Yasunaga

Founder & CEO, EiShin Co., Ltd. · Co-founder, ECO EFX Solutions Germany

Chie Yasunaga is the CEO and founder of EiShin — the idea creator and inventor of the NanoEFX fluid. She built her career in design and publishing, including work as publisher and editor at her father’s music-score business, and was already involved with environmentally friendly technologies in 2006, before joining EiShin. She has led EiShin Co., Ltd. since 2011 — the Japanese environmental-technology company behind NanoEFX, a natural-materials spray applied to vehicle air filters to support cleaner combustion, improved fuel efficiency and reduced emissions. Under her leadership, NanoEFX has been introduced internationally and is featured and supported by the United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO), the UN agency in Vienna promoting cleaner energy and sustainable industrial development.

Sven Oliver Robertson

CEO & Founder, ECO EFX Solutions Germany

The driving force behind NanoEFX. Sven Oliver Robertson is a highly skilled businessman with decades of experience in the global trade of consumer goods. For over two decades he has successfully sourced innovative products and advised global companies as a consultant, helping them expand operations and identify new business areas. His market knowledge, negotiation and contract expertise, and modern marketing skills, position NanoEFX at the forefront of environmentally friendly innovation — a pioneer in sustainable solutions for more efficient combustion engines with lower harmful emissions.

Khethi Nkosi

CEO & Founder, ECO EFX Solutions SA

Khethi holds diplomas in IT and in Project Management & Marketing, numerous leadership certificates, and is an NLP Master Practitioner. He worked for IBM, Consology, BCX and Attachmate NetIQ before founding xTelekom in 2012, and brings more than 20 years of ICT-industry experience. With his visionary approach he has helped unlock the potential of many companies, combining strategic thinking with practical solutions to grow sales, expand brand presence and meet regulatory requirements — an indispensable partner for NanoEFX.

Fintan Early

CPO & Co-founder, ECO EFX Solutions Germany

After a remarkable 27-year journey in the automotive industry, Fintan Early is a leading figure in engine and automotive engineering, combining deep technical expertise with business strategy and cultural competence. As Head of Business Development at Hinduja Tech for Japan, he sits at the interface between cutting-edge technology and sustainable mobility. As co-founder of NanoEFX, his experience ensures the company thrives at the intersection of advanced technology and automotive sustainability.

Michael R. Borg-Moeller

COO & Co-founder, ECO EFX Solutions Germany

Idea generator and visionary, with a quarter-century of experience in international sales and business and a 20-year track record of finding and developing innovative products. His background spans health, technology, fashion and more — a strategic go-getter who not only spots trends but shapes them, behind innovations such as ENCER, MyAirShield and MyPocketDoctor. His drive and commitment to global wellbeing fuel NanoEFX’s mission to transform engine efficiency and reduce environmental impact.

Jesper Juel

CTO & Co-founder, ECO EFX Solutions Germany · CEO & Founder, ECO EFX Solutions Scandinavia

With a strong international industrial background and deep experience delivering technical solutions in complex offshore and maritime environments — where safety, stable operations and full documentation are critical — Jesper Juel is a leading force. Trained as a shipbuilder and an expert in exhaust-gas cleaning systems, he led the development, production and implementation of scrubber technology in maritime shipping. His career and technical knowledge of pollution reduction make him indispensable to the ongoing development of NanoEFX.

Takudzwa Keith Chimeto

CEO & Founder, ECO EFX Solutions Zimbabwe

Takudzwa Keith Chimeto is an African tech entrepreneur passionate about building systems that let people do business digitally and securely. As founder of Smatpay and other platforms focused on fintech, digital-media monetisation and scalable infrastructure, he has built a strong network across major industries. His passion for innovation, sustainability and cost efficiency led him to ECO EFX Solutions — making him an exceptional partner in the family.

FAQ

Questions operators ask

Straight answers on how NanoEFX is applied, where it works, and how the risk-free pilot works.

Does NanoEFX require any engine modification?
No. It is applied to your existing air filter — no hardware, no ECU changes, no mechanical work, and no downtime.
How is it applied, and how long does it take?
Your own team sprays it onto the air filter. Typically 20–45 minutes depending on the vehicle or engine. Let it dry for at least 20 minutes, refit the filter, and run the engine.
How often does it need to be reapplied?
For cars, trucks and buses, roughly every 5,000–12,000 km. For ships and generators it depends on run-hours and size, so it is calculated per asset.
Which engines and fuels does it work with?
Diesel, petrol, LPG, CNG/LNG, biogas, biodiesel, hybrid ICE, 2- and 4-stroke, turbocharged or naturally aspirated — anywhere there is treatable intake filtration (paper, PET, felt, washable or acoustic media).
Is it safe and compliant?
Yes — REACH compliant and water-based, and backed by €5M product-liability insurance with zero claims since 2014.
What happens if it does not save fuel?
The pilot is risk-free: you measure against your own baseline and pay only if it proves fuel savings.
Risk-free pilot

Prove it on your own fleet — pay only if it saves fuel.

Run a controlled pilot on 5–10 trucks or buses, or 1–3 vessels, measured against your own baseline. Tell us about your fleet and we'll set up a 30-minute call — no commitment, and you only pay if it proves fuel savings.

  • Run a controlled pilot on 5–10 trucks or buses, or 1–3 vessels.
  • Measured against your own baseline — you pay only if it proves savings.
  • No engine modifications, no downtime, no risk to warranty.
  • UNIDO recognised · patented technology · backed by €5M insurance.
